test_cases.title: Successful behaviour: system accepts valid scenario for The system supports the following behaviour: Login Functionality

test_cases.title: Failure handling: system rejects invalid or unauthorised scenario for The system supports the following behaviour: Login Functionality
preconditions: Implementation for Jira issue ZT-3 is deployed to the test environment.
Tester has access to the application area related to: Login Functionality.

steps.action: Navigate to the feature related to: Login Functionality.
steps.expected_result: Feature can be accessed by an authorised tester.

steps.action: Attempt to violate the acceptance criterion by providing invalid or missing data for: The system supports the following behaviour: Login Functionality.
steps.test_data: Use clearly invalid, out-of-contract, or unauthorised values.
steps.expected_result: The system rejects the input gracefully, provides helpful feedback, and does not corrupt state.

steps.action: Observe logs or error messages if available.
steps.expected_result: Errors are logged appropriately without exposing sensitive details to the end user.

test_cases.title: Boundary conditions: limits and edge cases covered for The system supports the following behaviour: Login Functionality
preconditions: Implementation for Jira issue ZT-3 is deployed to the test environment.
Tester has access to the application area related to: Login Functionality.

steps.action: Identify boundary values for inputs involved in: The system supports the following behaviour: Login Functionality.
steps.test_data: Min, max, just-below, and just-above boundary values.
steps.expected_result: Boundary values are clearly identified for all relevant fields or parameters.

steps.action: Execute the operation using each boundary value in isolation.
steps.expected_result: The system handles all boundary values without crashes or data loss.

steps.action: Execute a combined scenario using multiple boundary values together.
steps.expected_result: Combined boundary conditions still meet the intent of the acceptance criterion or fail safely with clear messaging.
